doc.faq_section_long_desc: FAQSection is a component for listing frequently asked questions (FAQs) and laying them out as a section. It internaly uses Accordion component to provide a consistent user experience.
doc.faq_section_design_intent: FAQ sections are crucial for quickly resolving user queries on websites or applications. This component goes beyond just listing Accordions by acting as a 'section' with appropriate headings and descriptions.
doc.faq_section_choice_1: When there are many FAQs or you want to place a detailed description at the top
doc.faq_section_choice_2: When you want to utilize white space and make the title stand out on desktop screens
doc.faq_section_choice_3: When you want to place it as part of a page (such as a widget)
doc.faq_section_layout_left_desc: On desktop and larger screens, the title and accordion are lined up side-by-side. On mobile, they are automatically stacked vertically.
doc.faq_section_a11y_2: Appropriate level (h2) is specified for heading.
doc.faq_section_a11y_3: Keyboard operations (Open/Close with Enter, Space, navigate with arrow keys) are possible.
doc.faq_section_responsive_1: When layout="left" is specified, it becomes a 2-column layout on desktop (lg breakpoint and above), and the title follows scroll (Sticky).
doc.faq_section_responsive_2: On mobile devices, all layouts fall back to 1 column.
doc.faq_section_i18n_desc: Text passed as Props should be translated by receiving side using useTranslation etc., or specify a translation key.
doc.faq_section_content_guidelines_1: Write questions from the user's perspective, using natural phrasing (e.g. "How do I...?").
doc.faq_section_content_guidelines_2: Keep answers concise and focused on a single topic. Use links for detailed explanations.
doc.faq_section_content_guidelines_3: Group related questions under a shared title and description to give users context.
doc.faq_section_motion_desc: Open and close animations are handled by the internal Accordion component. No additional motion configuration is needed in FAQSection.
doc.faq_section_best_practices_1: Limit the list to genuinely common questions; avoid adding rarely asked questions that add noise.
doc.faq_section_best_practices_2: Use `layout="left"` to make effective use of whitespace on wider screens.
doc.faq_section_best_practices_3: Always provide a `title` and `description` so users can understand the topic before browsing questions.
doc.faq_section_keyboard_desc: All keyboard interactions (open/close with Enter or Space, navigate items with arrow keys) are delegated to the internal Accordion component and require no additional implementation.
doc.accordion_a11y_spec: Properly set aria-expanded for triggers and aria-labelledby for content, and support navigation with keyboard arrow keys.
doc.accordion_anatomy_content: An area for detailed information or controls displayed when expanded.
doc.accordion_anatomy_icon: Usually a caret, indicating the state by pointing up when open and down (or right) when closed.
doc.accordion_anatomy_item: A single unit (pair of trigger and content) that makes up the accordion.
doc.accordion_anatomy_trigger: An area clicked to control opening and closing. Includes an icon showing the current state (expanded/collapsed).
doc.accordion_best_practice_1: Provide clear labels for triggers (headings) that make it easy to guess the content inside.
doc.accordion_best_practice_2: Avoid hiding critical notifications or mandatory actions that users must not miss inside an accordion.
doc.accordion_choice_accordion_when: When you want to arrange multiple related topics vertically, such as FAQs or detailed settings, and allow users to choose only what they need to read.
doc.accordion_choice_disclosure_when: In very simple cases like 'Show Details' where you just want to open and close a single fragment of information.
doc.accordion_choice_matrix_desc: Choose based on the priority of information and the space occupancy tolerance on the page.
doc.accordion_choice_tabs_when: When each topic is very important and the types of information are clearly separated, and you want to switch or compare them quickly.
doc.accordion_content_guidelines: The trigger label should be concrete and short so that it is clear what will be displayed after expansion.
doc.accordion_design_intent: Accordion is used to lower the cognitive load on users by dividing a large amount of information or settings into logical groups and hiding them by default. Users can expand only specific sections as needed and focus on the information they are interested in.
doc.accordion_disabled_desc: By passing the `disabled` prop to an `Accordion.Item`, you can disable interaction with that item.
doc.accordion_features_1_desc: Choose whether only one item can be opened at a time or multiple items can be opened simultaneously.
doc.accordion_features_2_desc: You can click an already open item in `single` mode to close it.
doc.accordion_features_3_desc: Complies with WAI-ARIA and holds appropriate roles and attributes.
doc.accordion_guideline_1: Use when displaying all content at once would make the screen cluttered, such as in FAQs or supplemental details.
doc.accordion_guideline_2: Effective in limited spaces like mobile screens, as it allows users to choose and open only the information they need.
doc.accordion_long_desc: The accordion component allows content to be folded and displayed, letting users click to reveal more details.
doc.accordion_motion_spec: When expanding content, smooth changes in height make it easier to see the connection between information, rather than just appearing or disappearing.
doc.accordion_multiple_desc: By setting `type="multiple"`, you can have multiple items open at the same time.
doc.accordion_scenario_faq: FAQ: Use questions as triggers and hide answers to improve the overall view.
doc.accordion_scenario_filter: Search Filters: Group filter items by category and secure operation space by closing unnecessary filters.
doc.accordion_scenario_settings: Settings Page: Group detailed settings options by function to make the hierarchical structure easy to understand.
doc.appshell_a11y_2: Proper landmark regions for screen readers
doc.appshell_a11y_desc: Uses semantic HTML5 elements (header, aside, main, footer) for proper landmark regions. Supports keyboard navigation between sections.
doc.appshell_anatomy_content: The primary area where main content or children are rendered.
doc.appshell_anatomy_root: The outermost container managing state and layout.
doc.appshell_basic_layout_desc: A simple layout with just a header and main content.
doc.appshell_best_practice_1: Use AppShell consistently across your application to maintain a predictable user experience.
doc.appshell_best_practice_2: Test AppShell behavior across different screen sizes and input methods to ensure accessibility.
doc.appshell_bp_a11y_desc: Ensure proper heading hierarchy and landmark regions for screen readers.
doc.appshell_bp_centered_desc: For reading-focused apps, use `centered` with appropriate `maxWidth`.
doc.appshell_bp_mobile_nav_desc: Use navbar for mobile-first applications to improve thumb-reachability.
doc.appshell_bp_padding_desc: Use the `padding` prop to control spacing around main content for better readability.
doc.appshell_bp_perf_desc: Keep sidebar and header content lightweight for better initial load and transitions.
doc.appshell_bp_responsive_desc: Test your layout on different screen sizes to ensure a consistent experience.
doc.appshell_bp_semantic_desc: Combine AppShell with Header, Sidebar, Footer, and Navbar components for consistent styling.
doc.appshell_choice_alt_when: When a simpler alternative like Flex better suits the use case.
doc.appshell_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on your requirements.
doc.appshell_choice_self_when: When AppShell functionality with standard options is required.
doc.appshell_composition_desc: AppShell also provides sub-components for more granular control:
doc.appshell_content_guideline: Use semantic sub-components (AppShell.Header, AppShell.Sidebar) to ensure proper landmark regions and consistent theming.
doc.appshell_desc: A flexible application shell component that provides a complete layout structure for modern web applications. AppShell combines header, sidebar, main content, footer, and navbar areas into a cohesive layout system.
doc.appshell_design_intent: AppShell provides a foundational page structure orchestrating header, sidebar, main content, footer, and navbar into a unified responsive layout, eliminating repetitive layout boilerplate.
doc.appshell_feat_centered_desc: Option to center content with maximum width constraint
doc.appshell_feat_flex_desc: Compose your layout with any combination of header, sidebar, main content, footer, and navbar
doc.appshell_feat_padding_desc: Control padding on the main content area
doc.appshell_feat_responsive_desc: Automatically adapts to different screen sizes
doc.appshell_feat_semantic_desc: Uses proper semantic elements (header, aside, main, footer)
doc.appshell_motion_desc: Sidebar collapse/expand transitions animate width smoothly. No motion on the main layout itself.
doc.appshell_overview_desc: The AppShell component is designed to be the foundation of your application's layout. It handles the positioning and organization of major UI sections, allowing you to focus on building your application's features.
doc.appshell_resp_desktop_desc: Full layout with the sidebar visible by default.
doc.appshell_resp_mobile_desc: Sidebar is typically hidden, and a bottom navbar is shown for primary navigation.
doc.appshell_resp_padding_desc: Content padding adjusts automatically to maximize usable space on smaller screens.
doc.appshell_resp_tablet_desc: Sidebar may collapse into an icon-only view or become a drawer.
doc.appshell_scenario_1: Integrating AppShell into workflows for data collection or display.
doc.appshell_scenario_2: Using AppShell in management views for information organization.
doc.appshell_sections_footer_desc: Positioned at the bottom; typically contains copyright, links, and legal information. Uses the `Footer` component.
doc.appshell_sections_header_desc: Fixed or sticky positioning; typically contains branding, navigation, and user actions. Uses the `Header` component.
doc.appshell_sections_main_desc: Primary content area with optional padding and centering; scrollable when content exceeds viewport height.
doc.appshell_sections_navbar_desc: Bottom navigation bar for mobile applications, fixed at the bottom. Uses the `Navbar` component.
doc.appshell_sections_sidebar_desc: Collapsible navigation panel with configurable width; can be positioned on left or right. Responsive for mobile.
doc.appshell_with_footer_desc: Include a footer for additional information.
doc.appshell_with_navbar_desc: Perfect for mobile applications with bottom navigation.
doc.avatargroup_a11y_spec: Group the entire component with role="group" and assign an aria-label containing total count information, such as "15 project participants".
doc.avatargroup_anatomy_excess: Indicates the number of users exceeding the display limit in a count format like "+5".
doc.avatargroup_anatomy_items: Circular image elements representing each user. Usually overlap slightly to the right.
doc.avatargroup_choice_avatar_when: When showing only a single user, emphasizing a specific identity.
doc.avatargroup_choice_avatargroup_when: When you want to overlap multiple users and present a visual summary in a limited space.
doc.avatargroup_choice_matrix_desc: Select based on the number of users to display and design constraints.
doc.avatargroup_choice_tooltip_when: When you want to show many user names in detail via text to precisely convey who is participating.
doc.avatargroup_content_guidelines: Uniformly size avatars within the group. If there are too many elements, use the max property to limit the display count and reduce interface complexity.
doc.avatargroup_design_intent: AvatarGroups are used to display multiple users or entities gathered in one place. They compactly show a list of project participants or users who reacted to content, emphasizing the social context.
doc.avatargroup_long_desc: A component for displaying multiple avatars overlapping to visually represent a group or list of participants.
doc.avatargroup_max_desc: You can limit the maximum number of avatars to display. The excess will be shown as a number.
doc.avatargroup_motion_spec: Bring an avatar to the front or expand it on hover to encourage interaction with individual users.
doc.avatargroup_scenario_chat: Chat: Displaying several member avatars in combination as a group icon representation.
doc.avatargroup_scenario_collab: Collaboration: Displaying a list of members currently viewing or editing a shared document.
doc.avatargroup_scenario_sns: SNS: Overlapping previews of users who reacted to a post to visualize activity level.
doc.avatargroup_size_desc: You can apply sizes to all avatars within the group.
doc.avatargroup_total_desc: You can explicitly specify the total count regardless of the number of actual avatar elements.
doc.barchart_stacked_desc: By stacking multiple items vertically, you can compare both the total amount and its breakdown simultaneously.
doc.bentogrid_a11y_desc: Uses semantic list structure. Each grid item should have a meaningful heading for screen readers.
doc.bentogrid_anatomy_content: The primary area where main content or children are rendered.
doc.bentogrid_anatomy_root: The outermost container managing state and layout.
doc.bentogrid_best_practice_1: Use BentoGrid consistently across your application to maintain a predictable user experience.
doc.bentogrid_best_practice_2: Test BentoGrid behavior across different screen sizes and input methods to ensure accessibility.
doc.bentogrid_choice_alt_when: When a simpler alternative like Grid better suits the use case.
doc.bentogrid_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on your requirements.
doc.bentogrid_choice_self_when: When BentoGrid functionality with standard options is required.
doc.bentogrid_content_guideline: Use consistent card structures within the grid. Vary span sizes intentionally to create visual hierarchy.
doc.bentogrid_design_intent: BentoGrid creates a modern card-based grid layout (bento box style) for showcasing features, products, or content sections in a visually appealing arrangement.
doc.bentogrid_flexible_span_desc: You can use classes like wim-bento-grid-item--col-span-2 to make specific items span multiple rows or columns.
doc.bentogrid_modern_design_desc: Card format layout, suitable for showcasing product features.
doc.bentogrid_motion_desc: No animation by default. Individual cards may have their own hover transitions.
doc.bentogrid_prop_header: Content displayed at the top of the item (image, placeholder, etc.)
doc.bentogrid_prop_icon: Icon displayed next to the title
doc.bentogrid_responsive_desc: Automatically adjusts to 1 column on mobile and 3 columns on desktop (default setting).
doc.bentogrid_scenario_1: Integrating BentoGrid into workflows for data collection or display.
doc.bentogrid_scenario_2: Using BentoGrid in management views for information organization.
doc.bentogrid_subtitle: The BentoGrid component is used to create a modern, visually appealing "bento box" style grid layout.
doc.box_a11y_desc: Renders a div by default. Use the 'as' prop to render semantic elements when appropriate.
doc.box_anatomy_content: The primary area where main content or children are rendered.
doc.box_anatomy_root: The outermost container managing state and layout.
doc.box_best_practice_1: Use Box consistently across your application to maintain a predictable user experience.
doc.box_best_practice_2: Test Box behavior across different screen sizes and input methods to ensure accessibility.
doc.box_choice_alt_when: When a simpler alternative like Flex better suits the use case.
doc.box_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on your requirements.
doc.box_choice_self_when: When Box functionality with standard options is required.
doc.box_content_guideline: Use Box for simple wrappers and styling. For specific layout needs, prefer Flex, Grid, or Stack.
doc.box_design_intent: Box is the most basic layout primitive, providing a simple wrapper with style props (padding, margin, background, etc.) that map directly to CSS properties.
doc.box_long_desc:
Box is the most basic layout component in WIM UI.
It provides properties that wrap CSS properties (p, m, bg, etc.), allowing you to apply styles intuitively without writing inline styles.
doc.box_motion_desc: No animation by default; inherits transitions from child components.
doc.box_scenario_1: Integrating Box into workflows for data collection or display.
doc.box_scenario_2: Using Box in management views for information organization.
doc.card_footer_desc: Area for supplementary information or secondary actions.
doc.card_header_desc: Area for placing card titles or primary actions.
doc.card_with_header_footer_desc: Structure content logically using Card.Header for titles and Card.Footer for actions.
doc.center_a11y_desc: Renders a div by default. No specific ARIA requirements.
doc.center_anatomy_content: The primary area where main content or children are rendered.
doc.center_anatomy_root: The outermost container managing state and layout.
doc.center_best_practice_1: Use Center consistently across your application to maintain a predictable user experience.
doc.center_best_practice_2: Test Center behavior across different screen sizes and input methods to ensure accessibility.
doc.center_choice_alt_when: When a simpler alternative like Flex better suits the use case.
doc.center_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on your requirements.
doc.center_choice_self_when: When Center functionality with standard options is required.
doc.center_content_guideline: Use Center for simple centering needs. For complex layouts, use Flex with justify and align props.
doc.center_design_intent: Center is a simple layout utility that horizontally and vertically centers its child content using flexbox.
doc.center_feature_1_desc: Center elements with just a few lines of code.
doc.center_feature_2_desc: Maintains centering across different screen sizes.
doc.center_feature_3_desc: Adjust styles like padding and margin as needed.
doc.center_icon_center_desc: Ideal for centering icons within buttons or containers.
doc.center_inline_placement_desc: Use when you want to center text or icons inline.
doc.center_long_desc: A component for centering elements (horizontally and vertically) within their parent container.
doc.center_motion_desc: No animation by default; inherits transitions from child components.
doc.center_scenario_1: Integrating Center into workflows for data collection or display.
doc.center_scenario_2: Using Center in management views for information organization.
doc.chatui_bp_grouping_desc: Group consecutive messages from the same sender
doc.chatui_c_container: The main wrapper for the chat interface
doc.chatui_f_flex_desc: Messages can be positioned left or right
doc.container_a11y_desc: Follows WAI-ARIA guidelines with proper roles, labels, and keyboard navigation support.
doc.container_anatomy_content: The primary area where main content or children are rendered.
doc.container_anatomy_root: The outermost container managing state and layout.
doc.container_best_practice_1: Use Container consistently across your application to maintain a predictable user experience.
doc.container_best_practice_2: Test Container behavior across different screen sizes and input methods to ensure accessibility.
doc.container_choice_alt_when: When a simpler alternative like Box better suits the use case.
doc.container_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on your requirements.
doc.container_choice_self_when: When Container functionality with standard options is required.
doc.container_content_guideline: Keep content concise and relevant to the component's purpose. Follow consistent labeling conventions across your application.
doc.container_design_intent: Container provides a consistent, reusable pattern for centering content and limiting maximum width, reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.
doc.container_feature_1_desc: Set the maximum width using size properties like xs, sm, md, lg, or xl.
doc.container_feature_2_desc: Horizontally centers content by default within the available space.
doc.container_feature_3_desc: Removes width restrictions to let the container span the full width of its parent.
doc.container_fluid_desc: Displays content across the full window width without restrictions.
doc.container_long_desc: Detailed information and usage instructions for the Container component.
doc.container_motion_desc: Transitions are minimal and purposeful, respecting the user's reduced-motion preferences.
doc.container_scenario_1: Integrating Container into workflows for data collection or display.
doc.container_scenario_2: Using Container in management views for information organization.
doc.container_sizes_desc: Each container size has a different max-width based on design system tokens.
doc.container_title: A component to center content and limit maximum width
doc.datagrid_action_column_desc: Use the render function to add custom buttons or links (e.g., Edit/Delete) to specific cells.
doc.datagrid_api_col_desc_2: Label or JSX to be displayed in the column header.
doc.datagrid_api_col_desc_4: Whether the user can sort the grid by this column.
doc.datagrid_api_col_desc_5: Custom cell renderer function receiving value, row, and index.
doc.datagrid_api_col_desc_6: Horizontal alignment of the cell content: left, center, or right.
doc.datagrid_api_col_desc_7: Data field key used to extract values for this column.
doc.datagrid_api_col_desc_8: Whether to pin this column to the left side of the grid.
doc.datagrid_api_props_desc_1: An array of objects defining the grid's columns.
doc.datagrid_api_props_desc_10: Pagination settings including total pages and current page.
doc.datagrid_api_props_desc_11: Whether to display alternate row background colors.
doc.datagrid_api_props_desc_12: Whether to show borders around every individual cell.
doc.datagrid_api_props_desc_13: Whether to highlight rows when the mouse hovers over them.
doc.datagrid_api_props_desc_14: Whether the header remains fixed at the top during vertical scrolling.
doc.datagrid_api_props_desc_15: Fixed height for the table container. Scrollbars appear if exceeded.
doc.datagrid_api_props_desc_16: Maximum height for the table container.
doc.datagrid_api_props_desc_17: Message displayed when the grid has no data.
doc.datagrid_api_props_desc_2: The dataset to be displayed in the grid.
doc.datagrid_api_props_desc_3: Whether to show the loading skeleton overlay.
doc.datagrid_api_props_desc_4: Whether to show the checkbox selection column.
doc.datagrid_api_props_desc_5: An array of selected row keys.
doc.datagrid_api_props_desc_6: Callback function triggered when row selection changes.
doc.datagrid_api_props_desc_7: Unique key field used to identify each row (string or function).
doc.datagrid_api_props_desc_8: Current sort configuration (column key and direction).
doc.datagrid_api_props_desc_9: Callback function triggered when a column header is clicked for sorting.
doc.datagrid_api_props_desc_mobile_card: Mobile view: Switch to card layout.
doc.datagrid_basic_usage_desc: Standard implementation of DataGrid. Simply pass your column definitions and row data to display the grid.
doc.datagrid_bordered_desc: Enabling bordered adds lines between all cells, creating a spreadsheet-like appearance.
doc.datagrid_empty_state_desc: Displays a custom emptyMessage when the dataset contains no rows.
doc.datagrid_full_featured_desc: A comprehensive implementation combining sorting, selection, pagination, and custom action columns.
doc.datagrid_loading_state_desc: Setting loading={true} displays a skeleton screen while data is being fetched.
doc.datagrid_long_desc_1: DataGrid is a high-performance component for displaying large datasets with advanced features like sorting, selection, and pagination.
doc.datagrid_long_desc_2: It offers more advanced capabilities than the Table component, including column pinning and horizontal scrolling for complex data requirements.
doc.datagrid_note: To help you choose between Table and DataGrid, please refer to the comparison section at the bottom of this page.
doc.datagrid_pagination_desc: Divide large datasets into manageable pages. Configure page size, current page, and change handlers.
doc.datagrid_row_selection_desc: Enable the selection property to manage row checkbox selection. Supports multi-select patterns.
doc.datagrid_sorting_desc: Enable sortable on specific columns to allow users to toggle between ascending, descending, and unsorted states.
doc.datagrid_sticky_column_desc: Pin specific columns (like IDs or Names) to the left side so they remain visible while scrolling horizontally.
doc.datagrid_sticky_header_desc: Keep the header row visible at the top while scrolling through long lists of data.
doc.datagrid_striped_desc: Enabling striped adds a background color to alternate rows for better readability.
doc.datagrid_table_vs_datagrid_1: Best for simple data presentation. Ideal for readability when handling smaller datasets.
doc.datagrid_table_vs_datagrid_2: Built for complex, data-heavy applications. Includes built-in support for sorting, selection, and pagination.
doc.datagrid_table_vs_datagrid_desc: Choose the component that best fits your data volume and required interactivity.
doc.divider_best_practice_1: Use dividers sparingly — too many can make the interface feel fragmented rather than organized.
doc.divider_best_practice_2: Prefer spacing or background color differences over dividers when visual separation is already clear.
doc.flex_a11y_desc: Follows WAI-ARIA guidelines with proper roles, labels, and keyboard navigation support.
doc.flex_align_desc: Align items along the cross axis using center, stretch, start, or end.
doc.flex_anatomy_content: The primary area where main content or children are rendered.
doc.flex_anatomy_root: The outermost container managing state and layout.
doc.flex_basic_desc: Standard implementation of the Flex component for building flexible layouts.
doc.flex_best_practice_1: Use Flex consistently across your application to maintain a predictable user experience.
doc.flex_best_practice_2: Test Flex behavior across different screen sizes and input methods to ensure accessibility.
doc.flex_choice_alt_when: When a simpler alternative like Grid better suits the use case.
doc.flex_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on your requirements.
doc.flex_choice_self_when: When Flex functionality with standard options is required.
doc.flex_column_desc: Display items in a vertical column.
doc.flex_content_guideline: Keep content concise and relevant to the component's purpose. Follow consistent labeling conventions across your application.
doc.flex_design_intent: Flex provides a consistent, reusable pattern for flexible layout component using CSS flexbox, reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.
doc.flex_direction_desc: Control the flow of content using row (default) or column orientations.
doc.flex_feat_1_desc: Flex lines can be displayed horizontally or vertically.
doc.flex_feat_3_desc: Control whether the flex items wrap or not.
doc.flex_gap_desc: Easily manage spacing between child elements using design system tokens.
doc.flex_justify_desc: Distribute space along the main axis using properties like center, start, end, or space-between.
doc.flex_long_desc: Details and usage for the Flex component.
doc.flex_motion_desc: Transitions are minimal and purposeful, respecting the user's reduced-motion preferences.
doc.flex_scenario_1: Integrating Flex into workflows for data collection or display.
doc.flex_scenario_2: Using Flex in management views for information organization.
doc.flex_wrap_desc: Control how child elements wrap when they exceed the parent container width.
doc.footer_a11y_desc: Follows WAI-ARIA guidelines with proper roles, labels, and keyboard navigation support.
doc.footer_anatomy_content: The primary area where main content or children are rendered.
doc.footer_anatomy_root: The outermost container managing state and layout.
doc.footer_best_practice_1: Use Footer consistently across your application to maintain a predictable user experience.
doc.footer_best_practice_2: Test Footer behavior across different screen sizes and input methods to ensure accessibility.
doc.footer_choice_alt_when: When a simpler alternative like Header better suits the use case.
doc.footer_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on your requirements.
doc.footer_choice_self_when: When Footer functionality with standard options is required.
doc.footer_content_guideline: Keep content concise and relevant to the component's purpose. Follow consistent labeling conventions across your application.
doc.footer_dark_desc: Using the background="dark" property apply a dark background color.
doc.footer_design_intent: Footer provides a consistent, reusable pattern for bottom page section for links, copyright, and supplementary info, reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.
doc.footer_long_desc: Footer is a component placed at the bottom of a page. It contains links, copyright information, sitemaps, etc.
doc.footer_motion_desc: Transitions are minimal and purposeful, respecting the user's reduced-motion preferences.
doc.footer_scenario_1: Integrating Footer into workflows for data collection or display.
doc.footer_scenario_2: Using Footer in management views for information organization.
doc.footer_title: Footer section for bottom layout and supplementary info
doc.grid_a11y_desc: Follows WAI-ARIA guidelines with proper roles, labels, and keyboard navigation support.
doc.grid_anatomy_content: The primary area where main content or children are rendered.
doc.grid_anatomy_root: The outermost container managing state and layout.
doc.grid_best_practice_1: Use Grid consistently across your application to maintain a predictable user experience.
doc.grid_best_practice_2: Test Grid behavior across different screen sizes and input methods to ensure accessibility.
doc.grid_choice_alt_when: When a simpler alternative like Flex better suits the use case.
doc.grid_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on your requirements.
doc.grid_choice_self_when: When Grid functionality with standard options is required.
doc.grid_content_guideline: Keep content concise and relevant to the component's purpose. Follow consistent labeling conventions across your application.
doc.grid_custom_columns_desc: Flexible column configurations are possible using fr units or specific values.
doc.grid_design_intent: Grid provides a consistent, reusable pattern for CSS Grid based layout component for complex arrangements, reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.
doc.grid_feat_flex_desc: Easily adjust column/row counts, gaps, alignment, and more.
doc.grid_feat_semantic_desc: Uses a div element by default and supports various HTML attributes.
doc.grid_long_desc: The Grid component simplifies implementing CSS Grid layouts.
doc.grid_motion_desc: Transitions are minimal and purposeful, respecting the user's reduced-motion preferences.
doc.grid_responsive_desc: Specify column counts per breakpoint using an object.
doc.grid_scenario_1: Integrating Grid into workflows for data collection or display.
doc.grid_scenario_2: Using Grid in management views for information organization.
doc.grid_subtitle: A component to easily implement CSS Grid layouts.
doc.group_a11y_desc: Follows WAI-ARIA guidelines with proper roles, labels, and keyboard navigation support.
doc.group_align_desc: Use the align property to control vertical placement (top, center, end, etc.) when elements have mixed heights.
doc.group_anatomy_content: The primary area where main content or children are rendered.
doc.group_anatomy_root: The outermost container managing state and layout.
doc.group_best_practice_1: Use Group consistently across your application to maintain a predictable user experience.
doc.group_best_practice_2: Test Group behavior across different screen sizes and input methods to ensure accessibility.
doc.group_choice_alt_when: When a simpler alternative like Flex better suits the use case.
doc.group_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on your requirements.
doc.group_choice_self_when: When Group functionality with standard options is required.
doc.group_content_guideline: Keep content concise and relevant to the component's purpose. Follow consistent labeling conventions across your application.
doc.group_design_intent: Group provides a consistent, reusable pattern for horizontal arrangement of elements with consistent spacing, reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.
doc.group_feat_align_desc: Defaults to align="center", aligning elements of different heights to the middle.
doc.group_feat_gap_desc: Adjust gaps between elements with the gap property.
doc.group_feat_grow_desc: Using the grow property makes all child elements expand to fill available space.
doc.group_grow_desc: When grow is set to true, each item receives flex-grow: 1, spreading equally across the container width.
doc.group_long_desc: Group is a layout component for arranging elements horizontally. It maintains consistent spacing by default and handles wrapping based on container width. Ideal for lining up inline elements like buttons.
doc.group_motion_desc: Transitions are minimal and purposeful, respecting the user's reduced-motion preferences.
doc.group_scenario_1: Integrating Group into workflows for data collection or display.
doc.group_scenario_2: Using Group in management views for information organization.
doc.group_title: Layout component to arrange elements horizontally
doc.header_a11y_desc: Follows WAI-ARIA guidelines with proper roles, labels, and keyboard navigation support.
doc.header_anatomy_content: The primary area where main content or children are rendered.
doc.header_anatomy_root: The outermost container managing state and layout.
doc.header_basic_desc: Place `Header.Section` inside the `Header` component. Use the `align` property ("start", "center", "end") on `Header.Section` to control content positioning.
doc.header_best_practice_1: Use Header consistently across your application to maintain a predictable user experience.
doc.header_best_practice_2: Test Header behavior across different screen sizes and input methods to ensure accessibility.
doc.header_bordered_desc: Using the bordered property adds a border at the bottom, helpful for clearly separating content.
doc.header_choice_alt_when: When a simpler alternative like Navbar better suits the use case.
doc.header_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on your requirements.
doc.header_choice_self_when: When Header functionality with standard options is required.
doc.header_content_guideline: Keep content concise and relevant to the component's purpose. Follow consistent labeling conventions across your application.
doc.header_design_intent: Header provides a consistent, reusable pattern for top page section for branding, navigation, and user actions, reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.
doc.header_glass_desc: Using the glass property applies a blur effect to the background, making it translucent and revealing content behind it.
doc.header_interactive_demo_desc: Use the Controls below to change properties like bordered, glass, and background in real-time.
doc.header_long_desc: Header is a general-purpose header component placed at the top of pages or sections.
doc.header_motion_desc: Transitions are minimal and purposeful, respecting the user's reduced-motion preferences.
doc.header_scenario_1: Integrating Header into workflows for data collection or display.
doc.header_scenario_2: Using Header in management views for information organization.
doc.header_sticky_desc: Using sticky or fixed properties allows the header to follow as you scroll.
doc.header_title: Header section for layout top and primary navigation
doc.infiniteScroll_access_desc: Important information should not be placed in the footer as it may become difficult to access; consider using it with a "View More" button.
doc.infiniteScroll_long_desc: InfiniteScroll is used to automatically load additional content when the user reaches the bottom of the page. It is a useful alternative to pagination for list-formatted data.
doc.infiniteScroll_perf_desc: To avoid excessive DOM elements, use in conjunction with virtual scrolling is sometimes recommended.
doc.inputgroup_a11y_desc: Follows WAI-ARIA guidelines with proper roles, labels, and keyboard navigation support.
doc.inputgroup_anatomy_content: The primary area where main content or children are rendered.
doc.inputgroup_anatomy_root: The outermost container managing state and layout.
doc.inputgroup_best_practice_1: Use InputGroup consistently across your application to maintain a predictable user experience.
doc.inputgroup_best_practice_2: Test InputGroup behavior across different screen sizes and input methods to ensure accessibility.

doc.inputgroup_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on your requirements.

doc.inputgroup_design_intent: InputGroup provides a consistent, reusable pattern for grouping input elements with addons, buttons, or labels, reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.
doc.inputgroup_motion_desc: Transitions are minimal and purposeful, respecting the user's reduced-motion preferences.
doc.inputgroup_scenario_1: Integrating InputGroup into workflows for data collection or display.
doc.inputgroup_scenario_2: Using InputGroup in management views for information organization.
doc.marquee_feat_custom_desc: Control duration, reverse direction, vertical orientation, and pausing on hover.
doc.marquee_feat_loop_desc: Automatically duplicates content for seamless scrolling.
doc.marquee_long_desc: The Marquee component is used to display content (text, images, icons, etc.) in a looping animation horizontally or vertically. Suitable for news tickers, logo scrolls, highlighting call-to-actions, etc.
doc.marquee_title: A component that scrolls content horizontally, often used for announcements or news tickers.
doc.masonry_a11y_desc: Follows WAI-ARIA guidelines with proper roles, labels, and keyboard navigation support.
doc.masonry_anatomy_content: The primary area where main content or children are rendered.
doc.masonry_anatomy_root: The outermost container managing state and layout.
doc.masonry_best_practice_1: Use Masonry consistently across your application to maintain a predictable user experience.
doc.masonry_best_practice_2: Test Masonry behavior across different screen sizes and input methods to ensure accessibility.
doc.masonry_choice_alt_when: When a simpler alternative like Grid better suits the use case.
doc.masonry_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on your requirements.
doc.masonry_choice_self_when: When Masonry functionality with standard options is required.
doc.masonry_columns_adj_desc: Adjust layout density by changing the column count.
doc.masonry_content_guideline: Keep content concise and relevant to the component's purpose. Follow consistent labeling conventions across your application.
doc.masonry_design_intent: Masonry provides a consistent, reusable pattern for waterfall-style grid layout for varying height content, reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.
doc.masonry_feat_auto_desc: Uses CSS column-count to automatically distribute elements into columns.
doc.masonry_feat_columns_desc: Specify any number of columns with the columns property.
doc.masonry_feat_spacing_desc: Easily adjust item spacing with the spacing property.
doc.masonry_long_desc: Masonry is a layout component that efficiently places elements of varying heights to fill gaps. Used primarily for image galleries and cards with mixed heights.
doc.masonry_motion_desc: Transitions are minimal and purposeful, respecting the user's reduced-motion preferences.
doc.masonry_scenario_1: Integrating Masonry into workflows for data collection or display.
doc.masonry_scenario_2: Using Masonry in management views for information organization.
doc.masonry_spacing_adj_desc: Adjust distance between elements with the spacing property.
doc.masonry_title: Masonry layout for efficient element placement
doc.patterns_header_desc: A header for administrative screens combining `Header`, `Input` (Search), `Badge`, and `Avatar`. It shows examples of responsive placement, user menus, and notification icon layouts.
doc.portal_a11y_desc: Follows WAI-ARIA guidelines with proper roles, labels, and keyboard navigation support.
doc.portal_anatomy_content: The primary area where main content or children are rendered.
doc.portal_anatomy_root: The outermost container managing state and layout.
doc.portal_best_practice_1: Use Portal consistently across your application to maintain a predictable user experience.
doc.portal_best_practice_2: Test Portal behavior across different screen sizes and input methods to ensure accessibility.
doc.portal_choice_alt_when: When a simpler alternative like VisuallyHidden better suits the use case.
doc.portal_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on your requirements.
doc.portal_choice_self_when: When Portal functionality with standard options is required.
doc.portal_content_guideline: Keep content concise and relevant to the component's purpose. Follow consistent labeling conventions across your application.
doc.portal_design_intent: Portal provides a consistent, reusable pattern for rendering content outside the parent DOM hierarchy, reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.
doc.portal_ex_mechanism_desc: Technique for rendering React components into a different DOM subtree outside of parent hierarchy.
doc.portal_ex_overflow_desc: Ensure floating elements are not cut off by containers with hidden exit or scroll overflow.
doc.portal_ex_target_desc: Render content into specific existing elements like document.body or custom containers.
doc.portal_ex_u1_desc: Displaying high-priority modal windows at the document root level.
doc.portal_ex_u2_desc: Ensuring tooltips and floating menus remain visible regardless of local overflow styles.
doc.portal_ex_u2_desc_2: Context-specific tooltips rendered at the global level for collision avoidance.
doc.portal_long_desc: Detailed information and usage instructions for the Portal component.
doc.portal_motion_desc: Transitions are minimal and purposeful, respecting the user's reduced-motion preferences.
doc.portal_props_desc_1: The React Node or element to be rendered through the portal.
doc.portal_props_desc_2: The target DOM element or selector where content will be teleported.
doc.portal_scenario_1: Integrating Portal into workflows for data collection or display.
doc.portal_scenario_2: Using Portal in management views for information organization.
doc.portal_u1_desc: Modals and lightboxes that need to span the entire application view.
doc.portal_u2_desc: Tooltips and popovers that must escape local scroll containers.
doc.portal_u3_desc: Secondary UI elements that should not affect the primary page layout flow.
doc.portal_when_to_use_desc: Guidelines on when to leverage portals for complex layout requirements.
doc.scrollarea_basic_desc: Standard implementation used to wrap content with custom, cross-browser consistent scrollbars.
doc.scrollarea_both_desc: Cases where simultaneous vertical and horizontal scrolling is required include:
doc.scrollarea_long_desc: Details and usage for the Scroll Area component.
doc.scrollarea_note_desc:
`ScrollArea` can control the scrolling direction with the `scrollAxis` property as `x` (horizontal), `y` (vertical), or `both` (both directions).
When horizontal space is needed, such as for wide tables or images, enable horizontal scrolling by specifying `scrollAxis="x"` or `"both"`.
The default is `y` (vertical only).
doc.scrollarea_simple_desc: For normal text or simple lists, it is common to use only vertical (`y`) direction to maintain readability.
doc.scrollarea_uc_diag_desc: Complex illustrations where the full picture doesn’t fit on the screen.
doc.scrollarea_uc_map_desc: When there is a need to move coordinates freely.
doc.scrollarea_uc_sheet_desc: When both columns and rows are numerous.
doc.scrollprogress_basic_desc: Visual indicator (usually a top bar) showing how far the user has scrolled through a page or container.
doc.scrollprogress_desc: A component that visualizes the progress of reading a page.
doc.scrollprogress_feat_custom_desc: Supports color variations. Can be customized to match your brand.
doc.scrollprogress_feat_target_desc: Supports scrolling within specific elements in addition to the entire window.
doc.scrollprogress_long_desc: Shows how far the user has read the current page with a progress bar, typically at the top of the screen. Useful for providing a visual guide of reading progress in long documents or articles.
doc.scrollprogress_usage_2: Scroll monitoring within a specific container
doc.simplegrid_a11y_desc: Follows WAI-ARIA guidelines with proper roles, labels, and keyboard navigation support.
doc.simplegrid_anatomy_content: The primary area where main content or children are rendered.
doc.simplegrid_anatomy_root: The outermost container managing state and layout.
doc.simplegrid_best_practice_1: Use SimpleGrid consistently across your application to maintain a predictable user experience.
doc.simplegrid_best_practice_2: Test SimpleGrid behavior across different screen sizes and input methods to ensure accessibility.
doc.simplegrid_choice_alt_when: When a simpler alternative like Grid better suits the use case.
doc.simplegrid_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on your requirements.
doc.simplegrid_choice_self_when: When SimpleGrid functionality with standard options is required.
doc.simplegrid_content_guideline: Keep content concise and relevant to the component's purpose. Follow consistent labeling conventions across your application.
doc.simplegrid_design_intent: SimpleGrid provides a consistent, reusable pattern for responsive grid with fixed or auto-fit column counts, reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.
doc.simplegrid_ex_auto_desc: The number of columns changes automatically according to the screen width by specifying `minChildWidth`.
doc.simplegrid_ex_break_desc: The number of columns can also be explicitly specified for each breakpoint.
doc.simplegrid_feat_auto_desc: Automatically adjusts the number of columns based on the minimum width of child elements using the `minChildWidth` property.
doc.simplegrid_feat_fixed_desc: Fix the number of columns using the `cols` property.
doc.simplegrid_long_desc: The SimpleGrid component is a high-level component for easily creating responsive grid layouts.
doc.simplegrid_motion_desc: Transitions are minimal and purposeful, respecting the user's reduced-motion preferences.
doc.simplegrid_scenario_1: Integrating SimpleGrid into workflows for data collection or display.
doc.simplegrid_scenario_2: Using SimpleGrid in management views for information organization.
doc.skeleton_animation_desc: You can select no animation, pulse (blinking), or wave.
doc.skeleton_desc: Placeholder shapes that mimic the layout during content loading
doc.skeleton_loading_state_desc: You can press the button to see how the skeleton and the actual content are swapped.
doc.skeleton_long_desc: A Skeleton is a placeholder displayed while content is loading. By displaying shapes that match the layout of the actual content, it allows users to foresee the post-load structure, shortening perceived wait times.
doc.skeleton_shape_desc: You can select text, rectangle, or circle depending on the type of content to display.
doc.skeleton_title: Placeholder shapes that mimic the layout during content loading
doc.skeleton_usage_example_desc: You can combine multiple skeletons to create placeholders such as actual card layouts.
doc.splitter_a11y_desc: Follows WAI-ARIA guidelines with proper roles, labels, and keyboard navigation support.
doc.splitter_anatomy_content: The primary area where main content or children are rendered.
doc.splitter_anatomy_root: The outermost container managing state and layout.
doc.splitter_best_practice_1: Use Splitter consistently across your application to maintain a predictable user experience.
doc.splitter_best_practice_2: Test Splitter behavior across different screen sizes and input methods to ensure accessibility.

doc.splitter_constraints_desc: Limit the resize range of panels using the `minSize` or `maxSize` properties.
doc.splitter_content_guideline: Keep content concise and relevant to the component's purpose. Follow consistent labeling conventions across your application.
doc.splitter_design_intent: Splitter provides a consistent, reusable pattern for resizable split panels for adjustable side-by-side layouts, reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.
doc.splitter_long_desc: The Splitter is a layout component that allows multiple panels to be resized via drag operations.
doc.splitter_motion_desc: Transitions are minimal and purposeful, respecting the user's reduced-motion preferences.
doc.splitter_pc_only: Desktop Only: Resizing via pointer operations like drag-and-drop is primarily designed for desktop environments. Use on touch devices is not intended.
doc.splitter_scenario_1: Integrating Splitter into workflows for data collection or display.
doc.splitter_scenario_2: Using Splitter in management views for information organization.

doc.stack_anatomy_content: The primary area where main content or children are rendered.
doc.stack_anatomy_root: The outermost container managing state and layout.
doc.stack_best_practice_1: Use Stack consistently across your application to maintain a predictable user experience.
doc.stack_best_practice_2: Test Stack behavior across different screen sizes and input methods to ensure accessibility.

doc.stack_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on your requirements.
doc.stack_choice_self_when: When Stack functionality with standard options is required.
doc.stack_content_guideline: Keep content concise and relevant to the component's purpose. Follow consistent labeling conventions across your application.
doc.stack_design_intent: Stack provides a consistent, reusable pattern for vertical or horizontal element arrangement with consistent spacing, reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.
doc.stack_feat_flex_desc: Internal use of the `Flex` component allows for Flexbox properties such as `align` and `justify`.
doc.stack_feat_reset_desc: Specify `row` or `column` via the `direction` property (defaults to `column`).
doc.stack_feat_space_desc: Adjust spacing by specifying tokens like `xs`, `sm`, `md` or numerical values in the `gap` property.
doc.stack_long_desc: The `Stack` component is used to arrange elements vertically or horizontally and manage spacing between them.
doc.stack_motion_desc: Transitions are minimal and purposeful, respecting the user's reduced-motion preferences.
doc.stack_row_desc: Elements can be side-by-side by specifying `direction="row"`.
doc.stack_scenario_1: Integrating Stack into workflows for data collection or display.
doc.stack_scenario_2: Using Stack in management views for information organization.
doc.stack_spacing_desc: Spacing tokens from WIM UI (xs, sm, md, lg, xl, etc.) can be used directly.
doc.stack_title: A layout component for vertical or horizontal alignment
doc.table_sticky_header_desc: By combining the `stickyHeader` and `maxHeight` props, the header can be fixed at the top when scrolling.
doc.togglegroup_a11y_desc: Follows WAI-ARIA guidelines with proper roles, labels, and keyboard navigation support.
doc.togglegroup_anatomy_content: The primary area where main content or children are rendered.
doc.togglegroup_anatomy_root: The outermost container managing state and layout.

doc.togglegroup_best_practice_2: Test ToggleGroup behavior across different screen sizes and input methods to ensure accessibility.

doc.togglegroup_content_guideline: Keep content concise and relevant to the component's purpose. Follow consistent labeling conventions across your application.
doc.togglegroup_design_intent: ToggleGroup provides a consistent, reusable pattern for toggleable button group for single or multiple selections, reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.
doc.togglegroup_group: When binary toggle states (On / Off) are grouped together.
doc.togglegroup_icon_only_desc: Useful for toolbars where space is limited and icons are universally understood.
doc.togglegroup_long_desc: ToggleGroup components allow users to select one or multiple options from a set of buttons. It's often used for formatting options, alignment, or filtering where a button-like interface is preferred over checkboxes or radio buttons.
doc.togglegroup_motion_desc: Transitions are minimal and purposeful, respecting the user's reduced-motion preferences.
doc.togglegroup_multi_desc: Similar to a CheckboxGroup but with a button visual.
doc.togglegroup_reason_1: When you need a button-like interface for selection.
doc.togglegroup_scenario_1: Integrating ToggleGroup into workflows for data collection or display.
doc.togglegroup_scenario_2: Using ToggleGroup in management views for information organization.
doc.togglegroup_single_desc: Similar to a RadioGroup but with a button visual.
doc.togglegroup_toolbar: For toolbars (e.g., text formatting: Bold, Italic, Underline).
doc.transition_desc: Utilities for managing smooth enter and leave animations with CSS classes
doc.transition_fade_desc: A simple fade-in/fade-out using opacity changes. Includes width animation when button labels change.
doc.transition_feat_1_desc: Coordinates classes like `enter` (during transition), `enterFrom` (start), `enterTo` (end), and `leave` series.
doc.transition_feat_2_desc: Automatically unmount child elements after the leave animation is complete.
doc.transition_feat_3_desc: Correctly supports React's lifecycle and hydration.
doc.transition_long_desc: The `Transition` component is a utility for easily adding transition animations (enter/leave) to components using CSS classes. It applies and manages classes at appropriate times according to each stage of the transition.
doc.transition_slide_desc: An example combining opacity and position (transform) transitions.
doc.transition_stage_enterFrom_timing: Applied at the start of the enter phase (before the first frame).
doc.transition_stage_enterTo_timing: Applied at the end of the enter phase.
doc.transition_stage_enter_timing: Applied throughout the entire enter phase.
doc.transition_stage_leaveFrom_timing: Applied at the start of the leave phase.
doc.transition_stage_leaveTo_timing: Applied at the end of the leave phase.
doc.transition_stage_leave_timing: Applied throughout the entire leave phase.
doc.transition_title: Utilities for managing smooth enter and leave animations with CSS classes
doc.treeview_checkbox_mode_desc: When the `checkable` property is enabled, a checkbox is displayed for each item, allowing for multiple item selections.
doc.treeselect_cascade_desc: Selecting a parent automatically selects all its descendants. If only some children are selected, the parent shows an indeterminate state. Use this when you want to select an entire category at once.
doc.treeselect_exclusive_desc: A parent and its descendants cannot be selected at the same time. Selecting a parent clears all its descendants; selecting a child clears its ancestors. Use this when selections at different granularity levels should be mutually exclusive (e.g. preventing simultaneous selection of "Year" and "Month").
doc.accordion_keyboard_desc: Tab moves focus through accordion triggers. Enter or Space toggles the focused item open or closed. Arrow keys are not required but the trigger order follows DOM order.
doc.accordion_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the Accordion.Trigger and Accordion.Content children.
doc.appshell_keyboard_desc: This component is a layout shell. Keyboard navigation applies to the interactive elements placed within each slot (header, sidebar, main, footer).
doc.appshell_i18n_desc: Apply i18n to the content placed inside each AppShell slot (header, sidebar, main, footer).
doc.bentogrid_keyboard_desc: This component is a layout primitive with no keyboard interaction of its own. Apply keyboard navigation to interactive content placed inside each item.
doc.bentogrid_i18n_desc: Apply i18n to the content placed inside each BentoGrid item.
doc.box_keyboard_desc: This component is a layout primitive with no keyboard interaction of its own. Apply keyboard navigation to interactive content placed inside it.
doc.box_i18n_desc: Apply i18n to the content placed inside the Box component.
doc.center_keyboard_desc: This component is a layout primitive with no keyboard interaction of its own. Apply keyboard navigation to interactive content placed inside it.
doc.center_i18n_desc: Apply i18n to the content placed inside the Center component.
doc.container_keyboard_desc: This component is a layout container with no keyboard interaction of its own. Apply keyboard navigation to interactive content placed inside it.
doc.container_i18n_desc: Apply i18n to the content placed inside the Container component.
doc.flex_keyboard_desc: This component is a layout primitive with no keyboard interaction of its own. Apply keyboard navigation to interactive content placed inside it.
doc.flex_i18n_desc: Apply i18n to the content placed inside the Flex component.
doc.footer_keyboard_desc: Tab moves focus through interactive links and buttons within the footer in DOM order.
doc.footer_i18n_desc: Apply i18n to the content placed inside the Footer component.
doc.grid_keyboard_desc: This component is a layout primitive with no keyboard interaction of its own. Apply keyboard navigation to interactive content placed inside it.
doc.grid_i18n_desc: Apply i18n to the content placed inside the Grid component.
doc.group_keyboard_desc: This component is a layout primitive with no keyboard interaction of its own. Apply keyboard navigation to interactive content placed inside it.
doc.group_i18n_desc: Apply i18n to the content placed inside the Group component.
doc.header_keyboard_desc: Tab moves focus through interactive links and buttons within the header in DOM order.
doc.header_i18n_desc: Apply i18n to the content placed inside the Header component.
doc.marquee_keyboard_desc: This component does not require specific keyboard interaction beyond standard Tab focus through any interactive content placed inside it.
doc.marquee_i18n_desc: Apply i18n to the content placed inside the Marquee component.
doc.masonry_keyboard_desc: This component is a layout container with no keyboard interaction of its own. Apply keyboard navigation to interactive content placed inside it.
doc.masonry_i18n_desc: Apply i18n to the content placed inside the Masonry component.
doc.portal_keyboard_desc: This component is a utility with no keyboard interaction of its own. Keyboard navigation applies to the content rendered through the portal.
doc.portal_i18n_desc: Apply i18n to the content rendered through the Portal.
doc.scrollarea_keyboard_desc: Arrow keys scroll the content area when it is focused. Page Up and Page Down scroll by larger increments. Home and End move to the beginning and end of the scrollable content.
doc.scrollarea_i18n_desc: Apply i18n to the content placed inside the ScrollArea component.
doc.scrollprogress_keyboard_desc: This component is a visual indicator with no keyboard interaction.
doc.scrollprogress_i18n_desc: This component has no text content. No i18n configuration is required.
doc.simplegrid_keyboard_desc: This component is a layout primitive with no keyboard interaction of its own. Apply keyboard navigation to interactive content placed inside it.
doc.simplegrid_i18n_desc: Apply i18n to the content placed inside the SimpleGrid component.
doc.splitter_keyboard_desc: Tab focuses the splitter divider handle. Arrow keys resize the panels when the divider is focused. Home and End reset to minimum and maximum sizes respectively.
doc.splitter_i18n_desc: Apply i18n to the content placed inside each Splitter panel.
doc.stack_keyboard_desc: This component is a layout primitive with no keyboard interaction of its own. Apply keyboard navigation to interactive content placed inside it.
doc.stack_i18n_desc: Apply i18n to the content placed inside the Stack component.
doc.togglegroup_keyboard_desc: Tab focuses the toggle group. Arrow keys navigate between options. Enter or Space selects the focused option. In single-select mode, selection moves with arrow keys automatically.
doc.togglegroup_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through each ToggleGroup.Item children.
doc.transition_keyboard_desc: This component is a utility animation wrapper with no keyboard interaction of its own. Keyboard navigation applies to the content placed inside it.
doc.transition_i18n_desc: Apply i18n to the content placed inside the Transition component.

faq.section_default_desc: Answering common questions about WIM UI.
faq.a1: WIM UI is a modern, accessible design system based on React. It features practical capabilities like container queries and multilingual support.
faq.a2: Simply install the package via npm and import the desired components. You can check the specifications of each component in the Storybook.
faq.a3: Yes. All components use semantic color tokens and automatically switch based on the system OS setting or the `data-theme` attribute.
faq.a4: You can customize it to fit your brand by overriding Sass (SCSS) variables or CSS custom properties (design tokens).

appshell.guide_desc: AppShell and its sub-components define the top-level frame of your application. Use the table below to understand each component's role and placement, then choose the right combination for your scenario.
appshell.shell_usage: Root container that manages layout slots and responsive behavior
appshell.header_usage: Branding, global search, user profile, and top-level navigation
appshell.navbar_usage: Mobile-first top navigation bar optimized for thumb reach
appshell.sidebar_usage: Primary navigation and hierarchical drill-down menus
appshell.footer_usage: Legal info, language switcher, and supplemental links
appshell.tabbar_usage: Mobile tab navigation for switching between top-level sections
appshell.few_features: Few main features, balanced desktop & mobile
